title: Using the MMU: Intel and OS Perspectives
summary: Explains how Intel hardware implements segmentation and pagination, how Windows and Linux actually use the MMU, and the MMU's overall benefits and costs.
content_points: Intel: segmentation cannot be disabled but can be made transparent (all segments cover entire memory).
Windows and Linux practice: segmentation not used (all segments = full address space); pagination with 4 KB pages (Windows also supports 4 MB).
MMU benefits: isolation between processes, hardware-enforced access checks, support for virtual memory.
MMU costs: complex management, memory for page tables, doubles memory access count (mitigated by TLB).
learning_objectives: Explain how Windows and Linux configure Intel MMU
